thesis: Pre-revenue critical minerals developer advancing the Elk Creek Project in Nebraska — North America’s only niobium/scandium/titanium project and the 2nd largest indicated rare earth resource in the U.S. Feasibility study confirms $2.8B NPV, 29.2% IRR, and 38-year mine life. Company broke ground on the $44.6M mine portal in March 2026 and has secured over $370M in funding including a $10M US DoD grant. Stellantis rare earth offtake term sheet adds strategic validation. Stock down 66% from 52-week highs, creating an attractive entry for patient investors betting on U.S. critical minerals security.

drivers: Project financing closes on schedule; construction advances to full-scale development
Stellantis offtake agreement finalized with strategic equity investment
Additional US government grants / loans secured (EXIM Bank, DFC)
Rare earth separation facility demonstrates commercial-scale recovery rates
Commodity prices for niobium, scandium, and rare earths remain elevated

base_case.assumptions: Construction progresses but faces modest delays. Partial financing secured with some dilution. First production pushed to 2028-2029. Stock re-rates as project de-risks but does not reach full feasibility valuation.

risks: Project financing fails to close; company runs low on cash and requires significant dilutive raise
Construction cost overruns or technical issues at demonstration plant
Niobium or rare earth prices collapse, undermining project economics
Regulatory or environmental opposition delays permitting
Competing projects (MP Materials, Lynas) capture market share first

catalysts.event: Project financing close and full construction decision
Stellantis definitive offtake agreement and potential strategic investment
Portal construction completion; underground development begins
EXIM Bank loan decision; additional government funding
First production of niobium, scandium, titanium

key_risks: Pre-revenue with multi-year path to first production
Project financing risk: $1B+ capex requirement may require significant dilution
Construction and execution risk on first-of-kind US critical minerals facility
Commodity price volatility for niobium, scandium, and rare earths
Competition from established producers and other US projects
